Browser tool · learning environment

CodeMixer
as a build system.

A browser-based coding studio that combines learning, building, visual editing, and export into one continuous workflow — helping users move from experimentation to real website output.

Explore project Back to studio

Core idea

Learning, building, and exporting in one place.

CodeMixer is designed to close the gap between reading about code and actually producing something with it. Instead of separating tutorial, editor, and builder, it combines them into one environment.

Code as a learning surface

The editor is designed to teach while the user works. Live preview, structured helpers, and built-in guidance reduce the jump between understanding and applying HTML and CSS.

  • ✓
    Live editor with immediate feedback
  • ✓
    Integrated snippets and learning helpers
  • ✓
    Built for experimentation without losing direction

Visual building without losing the code

A second mode makes structure-building easier for users who want a more direct visual workflow. The important difference is that the visual mode stays connected to the real code instead of hiding it completely.

  • ✓
    Visual section building
  • ✓
    Closer bridge between beginner and builder
  • ✓
    Designed to move back into code naturally

System logic

A workflow that moves from idea to output.

CodeMixer is not just a code editor. It acts more like a small website creation system that supports learning, visual composition, and export inside one environment.

Integrated cheat sheets

Instead of pushing users into separate documentation, the product brings HTML, CSS, color, effect, and structure helpers directly into the build flow.

Two modes, one product

Code Studio and Build Your Website are designed as complementary layers — one for structure and flow, one for precision and understanding.

2Modes in one product
LivePreview workflow
ZIPExport output
1Continuous learning space

My role

From idea to interaction logic.

CodeMixer brings together teaching structure, editor thinking, and product design. It's both a tool concept and a workflow system for making website creation more accessible.

What I worked on

Product Concept

Defined the two-mode product structure and the bridge between learning, visual building, and real output.

UX / UI

Designed the interface logic around code, helpers, layout, and the flow between editing and building.

Learning System

Focused on making code feel less abstract by embedding useful guidance directly into the working environment.

Frontend Prototype

Worked on shaping the experience into a usable browser-based product concept and prototype.

Deepwork Studio

A product-focused portfolio built around systems, workflows, and digital tools that move from concept to usable experience.
